idea-plugin.name: SeedBase
idea-plugin.description:
Generate realistic, foreign-key-consistent test data from your database schema, right inside your JetBrains IDE. Point it at a SQL dump, Django models.py, a Prisma schema, or a live Postgres/MySQL connection, and get a full dataset where every foreign key resolves and the data actually looks real, not "Premium Widget 1" and lorem ipsum.

Want to see the output first? Try the login-free sandbox in your browser: seedba.se/playground.

  • New Project from Schema: turn a workspace SQL dump, Django models.py or a Prisma schema into a project, DB type detected automatically, pick which tables to send, large dumps streamed
  • Push Schema: update a project from your schema files with a checkbox table picker
  • Generate Data and Preview Data: build a foreign-key-consistent dataset and view sample rows in a table inside the IDE
  • Insert Test Data at Caret: drop FK-consistent rows into the open file, formatted for the file type (SQL/PHP/Python/JSON)
  • Mock API and Upload OpenAPI: serve a project as a live REST API with a built-in Swagger Explorer, or bring your own OpenAPI contract
  • Pull a generation as SQL, CSV or JSON, or Push to Database (Pro/Team)
  • Secure login: browser-based authorization, token stored in the IDE's Password Safe

Getting started: create a free account at seedba.se (no credit card), then click the key icon in the tool window to sign in. The free tier is enough to generate full, foreign-key-consistent datasets for your schema.

More about the plugin: seedba.se/jetbrains.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.6.3
  • Marketplace listing now leads with what you get: realistic, foreign-key-consistent test data that does not look fake, plus a link to the login-free browser sandbox
0.6.2
  • Reset & Reseed now detects table names from INSERT-only exports, so the truncate step works even without CREATE TABLE statements
0.6.1
  • Local database workflow: load, reset and reseed against a local psql/mysql/sqlite connection (auto-detected from .env, docker-compose or schema.prisma), matching the VS Code extension
0.6.0
  • Reset & Reseed and Seed Test Data (generate + load): full database-workflow parity with the VS Code extension
  • Settings (Tools → SeedBase): configurable preview row count
0.5.x
  • Push to Database: load generated data into a configured connection (Pro/Team)
  • Preview Data: view sample rows in a table inside the IDE; wide tables scroll horizontally
  • Decluttered tool window: a slim toolbar plus a context menu; Pull SQL/CSV/JSON merged into one action with a format chooser
0.3.0–0.4.x
  • New Project from Schema with automatic DB-type detection, table selection and streamed large dumps
  • Insert Test Data at Caret, Mock API with a Swagger Explorer, and Upload OpenAPI to serve your own contract
  • Delete Generation; clearer error messages and a guard when generating with an empty schema
0.2.3
  • Clearer upgrade hint: when an export format is not available on your plan (for example Parquet on the Free plan), the error now points to upgrading to Pro instead of showing a generic failure. Free includes SQL, CSV and JSON.
  • Sign-in no longer briefly blocks the UI thread: the whole login (initiate + poll) now runs in the background.
0.2.2
  • Fixed a UI freeze: the credential store is no longer read on the EDT (tool window open and toolbar updates were doing a synchronous keychain lookup). Login state is now cached in memory and the token is read off the UI thread.
0.2.1
  • Clearer onboarding: the tool window and description now point to the free seedba.se account needed to sign in
  • Replaced deprecated platform APIs (credential store, list renderer) for forward compatibility with 2026.2+
0.2.0
  • Push Schema: turn workspace schema files into a SeedBase project schema — Django models.py (all apps combined automatically), Prisma and SQL, with a detect preview and confirmation before applying
0.1.0
  • Initial release: tool window with projects & generations, browser-based login (Password Safe), Generate Data, Pull SQL/CSV/JSON, Open in Browser
